hero.name: Fortunato Geelhoed
hero.tagline: Full-Stack Engineer & Technical Lead, 25 years in
hero.statement: I build, fix, and lead TypeScript platforms end to end. This site is built three times, in React, Vue, and Angular, to illustrate that the fundamentals carry across all of them.

services.label: What I do
services.title: What I can do for you

approach.label: How I work
approach.title: A few things I've learned to do.

frameworks.label: Frameworks
frameworks.title: Frameworks come and go. The craft compounds.
frameworks.intro: When I started in 2000, I was building with ASP, Flash, and table layouts. I've relearned the job every few years since.

services.title: Full-Stack Engineering
services.description: TypeScript from the database to the browser: React, NestJS, GraphQL and REST APIs, Nx monorepos, plus the CI/CD and containers around it. Built to run in production.

services.title: Frontend Architecture
services.description: Frontends built to outlast the team that started them. Design systems, micro-frontends shipped as packages, server-side and static rendering for performance.

services.title: AI Integration & Automation
services.description: MCP servers, agentic workflows, RAG over your own data, and self-hosted models for when privacy rules out the cloud.

services.title: Technical Leadership
services.description: Architecture calls, tech-debt strategy, design reviews, without stepping away from the code.

principles.title: Work it out before building it
principles.description: I figure out how the parts fit together first, so the design holds when the requirements move.

principles.title: Leave the team stronger
principles.description: Test coverage, static analysis, careful review, and sharing what I know. The aim is a team that's in better shape than before I arrived.

principles.title: Fast, but I own what ships
principles.description: I build with AI in the loop to move quickly, and I stay responsible for every line that goes out. I don't ship code I can't explain.

principles.title: Pay down the debt that matters
principles.description: Not all tech debt is worth fixing. I rank it by what actually slows delivery, so a refactor pays for itself instead of becoming the project.

proof.label: Full-stack project lead, Municipality of Amsterdam
Faster Tele2 Sim-Only page, beating Google's own AMP version
Published a library with next to zero prior Python experience
Worked for Dutch and Australian teams from the Netherlands, Australia, New Zealand, and Spain

writing.title: Specialization Is Overrated
writing.blurb: I don't know Python. So I published a library on PyPI.

writing.title: Too React
writing.blurb: A recruiter told me I was "too React" for an Angular role. So I built this site in all three.

cta.heading: I'd love to hear what you're building or fixing.
cta.label: Send me a message
